questions.question: Stateful microservice pattern (event-sourcing на Flink) — когда оправдан и в чём core advantage над traditional REST + DB?

options.text: Оправдан для core domain critical state machines (orders, payments, fraud, IoT shadows). Advantages: state IS the database (single-writer per aggregate root через keyBy — no races), full audit log из коробки (Kafka event log), replay capability из любой точки, EOS без 2PC между app и DB. Требует команду familiar с event-sourcing/CQRS/schema evolution discipline. Простой CRUD — overkill

questions.explanation: Event-sourcing + CQRS реализованные через Flink streaming engine. ProcessFunction = aggregate root с ValueState/MapState внутри. keyBy(aggregateId) — single-threaded per key, no concurrency issues. Replay — savepoint + earliest offsets для bug fixes / new projections. Schema evolution discipline критична (events живут вечно). Anti-pattern: simple CRUD без сложной business logic — REST + Postgres сheaper и проще.

questions.question: Three patterns для dim joins (broadcast / lookup / temporal) — когда каждый применять?

options.text: Broadcast: dim ≤ 100-500MB, rare updates (countries, feature flags, small catalog) — replicate full copy в BroadcastState HEAP per subtask. Lookup (Async I/O): big dim (GBs), current value достаточно (user profiles) — external Redis/Cassandra с Caffeine L1 cache. Temporal (FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF): historical correctness нужна для replay (currency rates, prices в billing) — versioned table с rowtime, FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF event_time

questions.explanation: Three patterns для different dim profiles. Broadcast cost = N parallelism × dim_size в heap — fast lookup (микросекунды) но bounded by memory. Lookup — unbounded dim size, async I/O capacity tuning critical, eventual consistency (cache TTL = stale window). Temporal — point-in-time correctness, deterministic replay, but state holds versions (TTL обязателен для cleanup). Production pipelines often combine: countries broadcast + users lookup + prices temporal.

questions.question: Flink CDC 3.6 YAML pipeline + schema.change.behavior=evolve — что критичный risk и mitigation?

options.text: Risk: destructive changes (DROP COLUMN, RENAME) auto-applied на live lakehouse — downstream queries сломаются, data loss возможен. Mitigation: production prod использует try-evolve (попытка с graceful fallback) + alerting на destructive operations + Postgres DDL governance с approval + lakehouse retention 30+ days для time-travel recovery + downstream queries handle missing columns gracefully (COALESCE)

questions.explanation: Schema evolution в CDC — powerful tool с обоюдным lezzo. `evolve` mode применяет ALL changes including DROP COLUMN на live production. Audit trail incidents: developer случайно DROP COLUMN customers.email в Postgres → automatic propagation → email column dropped в Paimon → BI dashboards SELECT email сломались → analyst team panic. Production guardrails: try-evolve (alert вместо apply для destructive), Postgres DDL governance, retention recovery window. Не accident-prevention, а accident-recovery design.

questions.question: ML_PREDICT inline в Flink 2.2 streaming SQL — production architecture для recommendation engine на 500k events/sec с budget constraints?

options.text: Multi-tier ML: 99% ONNX embedded inference (1-5ms latency, $0 per call) для pre-ranking; 0.9% smaller LLM batched (Haiku/GPT-3.5, 50 records/batch) для finalization; 0.1% GPT-4 для most critical decisions. Plus predictions cache TTL 1min для popular pairs. ML_PREDICT inline в Flink SQL — async batched, built-in retry/timeout/circuit breaker. Combined с VECTOR_SEARCH для semantic similarity. Все ML operations в одном SQL pipeline

questions.explanation: Production scale recommendation engine: 500k events/sec × premium LLM = $thousands/hour, latency 200ms — оба out of budget. Multi-tier solution: cheap ONNX для majority (99% events get pre-ranking), middle-tier (Haiku/3.5) для refinement (0.9%), premium (GPT-4) для critical (0.1%). Predictions cache massively reduces QPS на popular product-user pairs. ML_PREDICT inline снимает overhead отдельного ML microservice. VECTOR_SEARCH для embedding similarity. Все в одной job — Flink 2.2 disruption для streaming ML pipelines.

questions.question: Anti-pattern 'tiny state, huge cardinality' — какие mitigations и в каком порядке?

options.text: Order по impact: (1) Question if cardinality truly needed (re-architect granularity); (2) State TTL + cleanupInRocksdbCompactFilter — для bounded retention scenarios; (3) Probabilistic sketches (Bloom filter / HLL / Count-Min) для approximate accuracy OK cases; (4) Hash bucketing для unbounded cardinality с acceptable collisions; (5) External Redis с TTL для no-state-in-Flink; (6) Operator state если global, not per-key; (7) ForSt + GIC для recovery time independent от state size

questions.explanation: Anti-pattern fix involves multiple layers. Per-entry overhead 200-400 bytes в RocksDB × billions keys = TBs disk. Single magic switch обычно не работает. Decision tree: depends на (a) cardinality bound; (b) accuracy required (exact vs approximate); (c) retention requirement; (d) EOS constraint. Re-architect granularity часто overlooked но самый impactful. Probabilistic — для metrics OK с FPP 0.1%. External Redis — sacrifices EOS для constant Flink state. ForSt — recovery game-changer но не fix state size.

questions.question: Modern Hybrid architecture (Iceberg + Flink + Spark) vs Lambda vs pure Kappa — почему Hybrid стал default в 2026?

options.text: Iceberg/Paimon — primary storage shared между streaming (Flink) + batch (Spark) + ad-hoc (Trino). One codebase для streaming + batch over same tables. Hybrid Source (Iceberg backfill + Kafka live) для Kappa replay. Iceberg time travel для replay. Преимущества над Lambda: no двойная codebase, no merge complexity. Преимущества над pure Kappa: ML training naturally batch over Iceberg, regulatory batch reconciliation simpler, Kafka retention не financially expensive

questions.explanation: Modern Hybrid pragmatically combines best practices. Iceberg/Paimon как lakehouse — single storage layer; streaming + batch + ad-hoc query все share same tables. Flink streaming pipeline пишет в Iceberg (EOS). Spark batch ML training reads Iceberg. Trino ad-hoc. На bug fix: Flink Hybrid Source replay (Iceberg parallel backfill + Kafka live), atomic cutover. На regulatory: nightly Spark reconciliation. Lambda пытался решить same problem но через две системы — сейчас не нужно. Pure Kappa имеет проблемы с ML training и long Kafka retention costs.

questions.question: Streaming aggregation для billing (money-critical) — какой design pattern обязателен?

options.text: Только Flink streaming с EOS — достаточно

ОБЯЗАТЕЛЬНА batch reconciliation: streaming aggregate = fast estimate для dashboards, nightly Spark job re-aggregates raw events + computes corrections + overwrites authoritative reconciled table. Billing system reads ТОЛЬКО reconciled. Plus: allowedLateness 30-60min + side output для late events + alerting на drift > threshold + idempotent upsert sink. Это design discipline, не option
Streaming aggregations нельзя для billing вообще

questions.explanation: Critical mental model. Streaming aggregations имеют fundamental drift из множества источников (late events, watermark approximations, schema issues, code bugs). Для money — unacceptable без reconciliation. Architecture pattern: (1) Streaming Flink → Iceberg aggregate (real-time view); (2) Nightly Spark reconciles из raw events + compares + overwrites reconciled; (3) Billing reads ONLY reconciled; (4) Side output late events для investigation; (5) allowedLateness для reducing drift размер. Streaming = preview, batch = authoritative source of truth. Без этого даже 0.1% drift = тысячи $ ошибок на scale.

questions.question: Flink Agents (preview 0.2.1) — это production-ready replacement для LangChain/LangGraph для всех use cases AI agent applications.
questions.explanation: Не production-ready и не replacement. (1) Preview status: 0.2.x с breaking API changes, immature observability, small community. Production critical paths не должны depend полностью. (2) Не replacement: LangChain wins на maturity, community, rapid iteration, Python ecosystem. Flink Agents wins на production infrastructure (EOS, durability, scaling). (3) Production strategy: hybrid pilot — keep mature production tools для critical workflows, parallel pilot Flink Agents на narrow scope для learning. (4) Roadmap: 1.0 GA — Q4 2026 / Q1 2027. Use both wisely.
